description_long: This object is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, featuring a rich, dark wooden case with a rectangular profile and a protruding shelf at the base. The front face displays two prominent metallic bells, a circular mouthpiece, and a separate earpiece attached to the side by a metal hook. A crank handle is visible on one side, indicative of early manual telephone operation, while the opposite side has a metal bracket for the earpiece. The top of the unit has angular metal wire guides, likely for supporting the cord or for structural reinforcement. The overall design is ornate and functional, reflecting early 20th-century craftsmanship and technology.
description: A vintage wall-mounted corded telephone with a wooden case, metal bells, a crank handle, and a separate earpiece on a hook.
description_view_0: Side view showing the wooden case, protruding shelf, and the earpiece resting on a metal hook.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the two metallic bells, circular mouthpiece, and the shelf at the base.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with the crank handle and a clear view of the wooden casing and shelf.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the flat wooden back and angular metal wire guides extending from the top.
